vision_report.compositional_truth_check: Registry vs. Reality: P1 (Yellow Pot) is present but appears devoid of foliage. P2/P4 (Black Pot) contains a single, struggling specimen. P3 (Black Pot) contains the white rabbit anchor and a single leaf. The registry is largely inconsistent with the current biological state.

inventory_reconciliation.P2/P4: Systemic Loss (No evidence of Mexican Mint or Silver Guest)
inventory_reconciliation.P3: Partial Survival (Single leaf remaining with anchor)

plant_audit.P3: Single leaf remaining, showing signs of chlorosis or extreme stress.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Consistently dark and damp across all pots; no signs of crusting.
biome_observations.debris: Eggshell fragments present in P3; electronic component (header pin) introduced on the desk surface in later images.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_t4: Initial state shows a single leaf in P3. P1 and P2/P4 are already bare.
temporal_deltas.t4_to_t3: No significant change; P3 leaf remains stable.
temporal_deltas.t3_to_t2: Introduction of a white paper surface and an electronic component (header pin) next to the pots.
temporal_deltas.t2_to_current: The environment remains static; no further biological degradation or growth observed.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is in a state of terminal decline. P1, P2, and P4 are effectively dead/lost. P3 is the only pot with a living specimen, though it is highly compromised and likely non-viable long-term.
anomalies: Introduction of electronic header pin on desk surface.
Presence of eggshell fragments in P3 soil.
Persistent absence of registered plant species.
vision_report.narrative_description: The audit reveals a failed botanical environment. The registry does not match the visual evidence, as the majority of the plants have suffered systemic loss. The remaining specimen in P3 is isolated and shows poor vitality. The environment has been repurposed for non-botanical items (electronics/paper), suggesting a shift away from active cultivation.
